Off Calendar Sep-05-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jaime R. Kennedy of SEDGWICK LLP (415-781-7900), appearing for defendant / moving party Tosco Corporation
James P. Nevin of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / responding parties Larrell Fortner, et al.
(See sign-in sheet for all other appearances.)

RULING:
DEFENDANT TOSCO CORPORATION'S MOTION TO VACATE THE TRIAL DATE

No argument made and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Tosco Corporation's motion to vacate the trial date is granted. The trial date, which was July 15, 2013 with a current return date of September 9, 2013, is vacated. This case is added to the February 16, 2014 Trial Setting Conference. Discovery is reopened only as related to Tosco. Discovery remains closed as related to all other defendants. Plaintiffs do not oppose vacating the trial date or reopening discovery as to Tosco. If, however, new evidence is discovered during the course of the Tosco discovery, other defendants may bring appropriate motions to reopen discovery if good cause exists. As to defendant's request for an order to show cause pursuant to CRC 3.110(f), based on defendant's reply it appears that defendant has withdrawn this request. However, if such request still stands, the court denies the request as it is satisfied with plaintiffs' explanation why service on Tosco occurred in violation of the requirements of CRC 3.110(b). RULING:
DEFENDANT CRANE CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Crane Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 through 5 and granted as to issue 6. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Fortner was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Mr. Fortner's deposition testimony and plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. As to punitive damages, defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Kim Richardson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #5915 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)

RULING:
DEFENDANT COLLINS ELECTRICAL COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Collins Electrical Company Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Even if defendant sustained its initial burden, Dr. Schwartz's declaration creates a triable issue whether Mr. Fortner's colon cancer was caused by his exposure to asbestos. (Garrett v. Howmedica Osteonics Corp. (2013) 214 Cal.App.4th 173.) The information and explanation set forth in Dr. Schwartz's declaration were sufficient to support his opinions for purposes of opposing this summary judgment motion. (Id. at 187.).
